telegram.text: Hamas: Occupation Regime Commits Massacres in Gaza and Takes Advantage of World's Distraction. Hamas spokesman Hazim Qasim stated that the occupation regime has committed horrific mass atrocities against the residents of the Gaza Strip. He noted that the previous day, occupation forces attacked a group of civilians west of Gaza shortly before the evening fast. Furthermore, early Monday morning, artillery shelling targeted tents of displaced persons, resulting in the deaths of three women, including a journalist. Qasim emphasized that these actions represent a dangerous escalation and a grave violation of the ceasefire agreement. He also stated that the occupation regime is taking advantage of the international community's distraction by events surrounding Iran and Lebanon to escalate military action against the Gaza Strip and tighten the blockade, including the closure of border crossings, primarily the Rafah Crossing. He also added that the occupation regime enjoys the full support of the United States, which allows it to violate the ceasefire and continue attacks against civilians. In conclusion, Qasim called on international mediators to intervene to stop the Zionist regime's crimes against the Palestinian people in Gaza, cease violations, and urgently lift the blockade of the Gaza Strip. @khamasru

related_markets.title: March 9
related_markets.question: Will Israel strike Gaza on March 9, 2026?
related_markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Israel initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Gaza soil on the listed date Israel Standard Time (IST). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones, or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Israeli military forces that impact Gaza ground territory.

A strike on any area within the terrestrial territory of the Gaza Strip counts, including buffer zones.

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether they land on Gaza territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by Israeli ground operatives will not qualify.

The primary resolution source will be official government/military statements (Israeli or foreign), multilateral bodies (UN, etc.), or a consensus of credible reporting from major international media and national broadcasters/newspapers.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar date after this market's end date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.
related_markets.impact_reason: Headline discusses Israeli actions in Gaza, which could lead to future strikes.
